problem.title: Largest BST Subtree
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, find the largest subtree, which is also a Binary Search Tree (BST), where the largest means subtree has the largest number of nodes.
assumptions: Return the size (number of nodes) of the largest subtree that is a BST.
A single node is always a BST.

example.explanation: The subtree rooted at 5 (nodes 1,5,8) is a valid BST of size 3. Subtree at 15 is invalid (7 < 15 but in right subtree).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS
solutions.description: Use a post-order DFS where each call returns the min value, max value, and size of the largest BST rooted at that node. A node forms a valid BST extension when its value lies strictly between the max of its left subtree and the min of its right subtree. The key insight is propagating sentinel values (MAX_INT for empty left, MIN_INT for empty right) so boundary checks work uniformly at every node.
